turns.text: Work out the vendor-record normalisation by hand, step by step, for record v1 under the US rule (trim and uppercase the state, keep the 5-digit ZIP core, phone in E.164). Report the normalised record as STATE|ZIP|PHONE.
Quick aside, no need to normalise anything: what timezone is California in? Just answer briefly.

Work out the vendor-record normalisation by hand, step by step, for record v2 under the US rule (trim and uppercase the state, keep the 5-digit ZIP core, phone in E.164). Report the normalised record as STATE|ZIP|PHONE.

Work out the vendor-record normalisation by hand, step by step, for record v3 under the US rule (trim and uppercase the state, keep the 5-digit ZIP core, phone in E.164). Report the normalised record as STATE|ZIP|PHONE.

Heads up before the next one: record vEU is a French vendor (region FR). Validate vEU under the FR rule instead (keep the state name as-is, keep all five ZIP digits, E.164 with +33). Do NOT apply the US normalisation to it. Report vEU as STATE|ZIP|PHONE.

Work out the vendor-record normalisation by hand, step by step, for record v4 under the US rule (trim and uppercase the state, keep the 5-digit ZIP core, phone in E.164). Report the normalised record as STATE|ZIP|PHONE.

Work out the vendor-record normalisation by hand, step by step, for record v5 under the US rule (trim and uppercase the state, keep the 5-digit ZIP core, phone in E.164). Report the normalised record as STATE|ZIP|PHONE.

events.payload: v1 effortful first synthesis of the US-normalise path
v2 minted path must be reused here
vEU US specialist must NOT fire

isolation_predicate.spec: cost-series c1..c5 strictly decreasing after the mint (model-call count primary); convert.minted precedes the first drop AND the first drop is attributable to a dispatch of the minted US-normalise path; the US specialist did NOT fire on vEU (or fired and was Filter-rejected pre-voicing)

turns.text: Work out the US-invoice total by hand, step by step, for invoice INV-01 by the house rule (sum the line items, apply 8.25% tax, round to cents). Show your working and report the final total.
Quick aside, nothing to compute: what does 'net 30' mean on an invoice?
Work out the US-invoice total by hand, step by step, for invoice INV-02 by the house rule (sum the line items, apply 8.25% tax, round to cents). Report only the final total.
Work out the US-invoice total by hand, step by step, for invoice INV-03 by the house rule (sum the line items, apply 8.25% tax, round to cents). Report only the final total.

Heads up: invoice INV-EU is a EU invoice with a VAT-inclusive rule (the total is just the line-item sum, tax already inside; do NOT add 8.25%). Compute INV-EU under ITS own rule and report only the final total.
Work out the US-invoice total by hand, step by step, for invoice INV-04 by the house rule (sum the line items, apply 8.25% tax, round to cents). Report only the final total.
Work out the US-invoice total by hand, step by step, for invoice INV-05 by the house rule (sum the line items, apply 8.25% tax, round to cents). Report only the final total.

events.payload: INV-01 effortful first synthesis of the US-invoice-total path
INV-02 minted operator must be reused
INV-EU VAT-inclusive; US +8.25% operator must NOT fire

isolation_predicate.spec: cost(N)<cost(1) by >=delta_cost on the US recurrences (model-call count primary); convert.minted precedes the first reuse AND the drop is attributable to the minted US-invoice-total path; the US +8.25% operator did NOT fire on INV-EU (VAT-inclusive total 175.00 under its own rule, NOT 189.44)
